verses.text: 'Come, and let us return unto the LORD... he will heal us'; after two days He will revive us. A call to return. The God who wounds is the very One who heals the penitent.
'Come, and let us return unto the LORD... he will heal us'; after two days He will revive us. A call to return. The God who wounds is the very One who heals the penitent.
'Come, and let us return unto the LORD... he will heal us'; after two days He will revive us. A call to return. The God who wounds is the very One who heals the penitent.
'O Ephraim, what shall I do unto thee?' their goodness is 'as a morning cloud,' soon gone; so He hewed them by the prophets. Fleeting devotion. God grieves over love that fades as quickly as dew.
'O Ephraim, what shall I do unto thee?' their goodness is 'as a morning cloud,' soon gone; so He hewed them by the prophets. Fleeting devotion. God grieves over love that fades as quickly as dew.
'I desired mercy, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings'; yet they broke the covenant. Heart over ritual. God prizes a loving, knowing heart above all outward worship.
'I desired mercy, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings'; yet they broke the covenant. Heart over ritual. God prizes a loving, knowing heart above all outward worship.
Gilead is a city of evildoers; the priests murder by the way 'by consent.' Corruption of the holy. When even priests turn criminal, the land is far gone.
Gilead is a city of evildoers; the priests murder by the way 'by consent.' Corruption of the holy. When even priests turn criminal, the land is far gone.
A horrible thing is seen in Israel; and for Judah too 'he hath set an harvest.' Reckoning ahead. Both kingdoms will reap what their sin has sown.
